meta.name: Lamentations
meta.copyright: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: How lonely sits the city that once was full of people! How like a widow she has become, she that was great among the nations! She that was a princess among the provinces has become a vassal.
text.Rotherham: How is seated alone, the city that abounded with people, hath become as a widow,—She who abounded among the nations was a princess among provinces, hath come under tribute.

text.NRSV: She weeps bitterly in the night, with tears on her cheeks; among all her lovers she has no one to comfort her; all her friends have dealt treacherously with her, they have become her enemies.
text.Rotherham: She, weepeth sore, in the night, and, her tear, is on her cheek, She hath none to comfort her, of all her lovers,—All her friends, have betrayed her, have become her foes.

text.NRSV: Judah has gone into exile with suffering and hard servitude; she lives now among the nations, and finds no resting place; her pursuers have all overtaken her in the midst of her distress.
text.Rotherham: Carried away captive is Judah—because of oppression, and because of great servitude, She, hath remained among the nations, hath found no place of rest,—All her pursuers, have overtaken her, between straits.

text.NRSV: The roads to Zion mourn, for no one comes to the festivals; all her gates are desolate, her priests groan; her young girls grieve, and she her-self suffers bitterly.
text.Rotherham: The ways to Zion, are mourning, because none come to her appointed feasts, All her gates, are desolate, her priests, are sighing,—her virgins, are grieved, and, she, it is bitter for her.

text.NRSV: Her foes have become the masters, her enemies prosper, because the Lord has made her suffer for the multitude of her transgressions; her children have gone away, captives before the foe.
text.Rotherham: Her adversaries have become chief, her foes, are at ease, for, Yahweh, hath grieved her, because of the multitude of her transgressions,—Her children, have gone into captivity, before the adversary.

text.NRSV: From daughter Zion has departed all her majesty. Her princes have become like stags that find no pasture; they fled without strength before the pursuer.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath gone forth from the daughter of Zion, all that adorned her,—Her princes have become like harts that have found no pasture, and have gone strengthless before the pursuer.

text.NRSV: Jerusalem remembers, in the days of her affliction and wandering, all the precious things that were hers in days of old. When her people fell into the hand of the foe, and there was no one to help her, her foes looked on her; they mocked at her downfall.
text.Rotherham: Jerusalem hath remembered in the days of her humiliation and her fleeings—all her precious things, which have existed from the days of old,—Now that her people have been falling into the hand of the adversary, with none to help her, the adversaries have seen her, have mocked over her sabbath-keepings.

text.NRSV: Jerusalem sinned grievously, so she has become a mockery; all who honored her despise her, for they have seen her nakedness; she herself groans, and turns her face away.
text.Rotherham: Jerusalem, hath grievously sinned, For this cause, unto exile, hath she been delivered,—All who used to honour her, have despised her, for they have descried her unseemliness, yea, she herself, hath sighed, and turned back.

text.NRSV: Her uncleanness was in her skirts; she gave no thought to her future; her downfall was appalling, with no one to comfort her. “O Lord, behold my affliction, for the enemy has triumphed!”
text.Rotherham: Her impurity, is in her skirts, She hath not remembered her hereafter, Therefore hath she come down wonderfully, none to comfort her,—Behold, O Yahweh, my humiliation, that the foe, hath made himself great.

text.NRSV: Enemies have stretched out their hands over all her precious things; she has even seen the nations invade her sanctuary, those whom you forbade to enter your congregation.
text.Rotherham: His hand, hath the adversary spread out, over all her precious things,—for she saw that, the nations, entered her sanctuary, as to whom thou didst command they should not enter—in the convocation—unto thee!

text.NRSV: All her people groan as they search for bread; they trade their treasures for food to revive their strength. “Look, O Lord, and see how worthless I have become.”
text.Rotherham: All her people, are sighing, seeking bread, They have given their precious things for food, to bring back life,—Behold, O Yahweh, and discern, that I have become worthless.

text.NRSV: Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by? Look and see if there is any sorrow like my sorrow, which was brought upon me, which the Lord inflicted on the day of his fierce anger.
text.Rotherham: Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by? Look around and see, whether there is pain like my pain, which is severely dealt out to me,—in that Yahweh, hath caused grief, in the day of the glow of his anger?

text.NRSV: From on high he sent fire; it went deep into my bones; he spread a net for my feet; he turned me back; he has left me stunned, faint all day long.
text.Rotherham: From on high, sent he fire, among my bones, and laid them prostrate,—He spread out a net for my feet, he made me turn back, he made me desolate, all the day, faint.

text.NRSV: My transgressions were bound into a yoke; by his hand they were fastened together; they weigh on my neck, sapping my strength; the Lord delivered me into the hands of those whom I cannot withstand.
text.Rotherham: The yoke of my transgressions, hath been bound, by his hand—They have intertwined themselves, have come up on my neck, it hath paralysed my strength,—My Lord, hath delivered me, into the hands of those against whom I cannot rise up.

text.NRSV: The Lord has rejected all my warriors in the midst of me; he proclaimed a time against me to crush my young men; the Lord has trodden as in a wine press the virgin daughter Judah.
text.Rotherham: My Lord, hath flouted at all my magnates, in my midst, He hath called against me a host, to crush my young men,—A winepress, hath My Lord trodden, to the virgin, the daughter of Judah.

text.NRSV: For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears; for a comforter is far from me, one to revive my courage; my children are desolate, for the enemy has prevailed.
text.Rotherham: For these things, am, I, weeping, Mine eye, mine eye, is running down with waters, for, far from me, is any who could comfort, could bring back my life,—My sons are amazed, for strong is the foe.

text.NRSV: Zion stretches out her hands, but there is no one to comfort her; the Lord has commanded against Jacob that his neighbors should be his foes; Jerusalem has become a filthy thing among them.
text.Rotherham: Zion, hath spread forth, her hands, there is none to comfort her, Yahweh, hath given command, respecting Jacob, unto them who surround him—his adversaries,—Jerusalem, hath become as a removed woman, in their midst.

text.NRSV: The Lord is in the right, for I have rebelled against his word; but hear, all you peoples, and behold my suffering; my young women and my young men have gone into captivity.
text.Rotherham: Righteous is Yahweh, for, against his bidding, had I rebelled,—Hear, I pray you, all ye peoples, and see my pain, My virgins and my young men, have gone into exile.

text.NRSV: I called to my lovers but they deceived me; my priests and elders perished in the city while seeking food to revive their strength.
text.Rotherham: I called to my lovers, they, deceived me, My priests and mine elders, in the city, have breathed their last,—for they sought them food, that they might bring back their life.

text.NRSV: See, O Lord, how distressed I am; my stomach churns, my heart is wrung within me, because I have been very rebellious. In the street the sword bereaves; in the house it is like death.
text.Rotherham: See, O Yahweh, that I am in distress, Mine inward parts, are in ferment, My heart is turned within me, for I have obstinately rebelled,—Without, bereaveth the sword, Within, is like death,

text.NRSV: They heard how I was groaning, with no one to comfort me. All my enemies heard of my trouble; they are glad that you have done it. Bring on the day you have announced, and let them be as I am.
text.Rotherham: They have heard—that, sighing am, I, [and am saying] —There is none to comfort me, All my foes,—having heard of my calamity—have rejoiced, because, thou, hast done it,—thou hast brought in the day thou didst proclaim, So let them become like me.

text.NRSV: Let all their evil doing come before you; and deal with them as you have dealt with me because of all my transgressions; for my groans are many and my heart is faint.
text.Rotherham: Let all their wickedness, come in, before thee, and deal thou severely with them, according as thou hast dealt severely with me, for all my transgressions; for many are my sighs, and, my heart, is sick.

text.NRSV: How the Lord in his anger has humiliated daughter Zion! He has thrown down from heaven to earth the splendor of Israel; he has not remembered his footstool in the day of his anger.
text.Rotherham: How could My Lord, in his anger, enshroud in gloom, the daughter of Zion? have cast from the heavens to the earth, the beauty of Israel? and not have remembered his footstool, in the day of his anger?

text.NRSV: The Lord has destroyed without mercy all the dwellings of Jacob; in his wrath he has broken down the strongholds of daughter Judah; he has brought low in dishonor the kingdom and its rulers.
text.Rotherham: My Lord hath swallowed up—without pity—all the pastures of Jacob, hath laid waste—in his indignation—the strongholds of the daughter of Judah, hath brought them down to the ground,—hath profaned the kingdom, and the princes thereof.

text.NRSV: He has cut down in fierce anger all the might of Israel; he has withdrawn his right hand from them in the face of the enemy; he has burned like a flaming fire in Jacob, consuming all around.
text.Rotherham: He hath broken off—in the glow of his anger—the whole horn of Israel, hath turned back his right hand, from the face of the foe,—and hath kindled against Jacob, a very fire of flame, devouring round about.

text.NRSV: He has bent his bow like an enemy, with his right hand set like a foe; he has killed all in whom we took pride in the tent of daughter Zion; he has poured out his fury like fire.
text.Rotherham: He hath trodden his bow like a foe, his right hand erect as an adversary, and hath slain all them who delighted the eye,—In the home of the daughter of Zion, hath he poured out, as fire, his indignation.

text.NRSV: The Lord has become like an enemy; he has destroyed Israel; he has destroyed all its palaces, laid in ruins its strongholds; and he has multiplied in daughter Judah mourning and lamentation.
text.Rotherham: My Lord hath become like a foe, hath swallowed up Israel, hath swallowed up all her castles, ruined his strongholds,—and hath caused to abound, in the daughter of Judah, lamentation and mourning.

text.NRSV: He has broken down his booth like a garden, he has destroyed his tabernacle; the Lord has abolished in Zion festival and sabbath, and in his fierce indignation has spurned king and priest.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath he destroyed, like a garden, his pavilion, hath laid waste his place of assembly,—Yahweh, hath caused to be forgotten, in Zion, the appointed assembly and the sabbath, and hath spurned, in the indignation of his anger, the king and the priest.

text.NRSV: The Lord has scorned his altar, disowned his sanctuary; he has delivered into the hand of the enemy the walls of her palaces; a clamor was raised in the house of the Lord as on a day of festival.
text.Rotherham: My Lord hath rejected his altar, hath abhorred his sanctuary, hath delivered, into the hand of the foe, the walls of her castles,—A voice, have they uttered in the house of Yahweh, as on the day of an appointed assembly.

text.NRSV: The Lord determined to lay in ruins the wall of daughter Zion; he stretched the line; he did not stay his hand from destroying; he caused rampart and wall to lament; they languish together.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath devised to lay in ruins, the wall of the daughter of Zion, he hath stretched out a line, he hath not turned back his hand from swallowing up,—Thus hath he caused to mourn—rampart and wall, together, have they languished!

text.NRSV: Her gates have sunk into the ground; he has ruined and broken her bars; her king and her princes are among the nations; guidance is no more, and her prophets obtain no vision from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Her gates, have sunk in the earth, He hath destroyed and broken in pieces, her bars,—Her king and her princes, are among the nations, There is no instruction, Even her prophets, have found no vision, from Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The elders of daughter Zion sit on the ground in silence; they have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth; the young girls of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
text.Rotherham: Seated on the ground, in silence, are the elders of the daughter of Zion, They have lifted up dust on their head, have girded themselves with sackcloth,—Bowed down to the ground is the head, of the virgins of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: My eyes are spent with weeping; my stomach churns; my bile is poured out on the ground because of the destruction of my people, because infants and babes faint in the streets of the city.
text.Rotherham: Blinded with tears are mine eyes, In ferment is my body, Poured out to the earth is my grief, for the sore hurt of the daughter of my people,—when child and suckling are swooning, in the broadways of the city.

text.NRSV: They cry to their mothers, “Where is bread and wine?” as they faint like the wounded in the streets of the city, as their life is poured out on their mothers’ bosom.
text.Rotherham: To their mothers, they keep saying, Where are corn and wine? Swooning off, like one thrust through, in the broadways of the city, pouring out their life into the bosom of their mothers.

text.NRSV: What can I say for you, to what compare you, O daughter Jerusalem? To what can I liken you, that I may comfort you, O virgin daughter Zion? For vast as the sea is your ruin; who can heal you?
text.Rotherham: How shall I solemnly admonish thee? What shall I liken to thee, thou daughter of Jerusalem? What shall I compare with thee, that I may comfort thee, thou virgin, daughter of Zion? for, great as the sea, is thy grievous injury, Who can heal thee?

text.NRSV: Your prophets have seen for you false and deceptive visions; they have not exposed your iniquity to restore your fortunes, but have seen oracles for you that are false and misleading.
text.Rotherham: Thy prophets, have had visions for thee, false and foolish, and have not unveiled thine iniquity, to turn back thy captivity,—Yea they have had visions for thee, oracles of falsehood, and enticements!

text.NRSV: All who pass by snap their fingers at you; they hiss and wag their heads at daughter Jerusalem; “Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty, the joy of all the earth?”
text.Rotherham: All passing by, have clapped, over thee, their hands, have hissed and wagged their head over the daughter of Jerusalem,—[saying], Is, this, the city, of which men used to say—The perfection of beauty! A joy to the whole earth!

text.NRSV: All your enemies open their mouths against you; they hiss, they gnash their teeth, they cry: “We have devoured her! Ah, this is the day we longed for; at last we have seen it!”
text.Rotherham: All thy foes, have opened wide, over thee their mouth, They have hissed, and gnashed their teeth, They have said—We have swallowed [her] up! Surely, this, is the day for which we have waited, We have found! We have seen!

text.NRSV: The Lord has done what he purposed, he has carried out his threat; as he ordained long ago, he has demolished without pity; he has made the enemy rejoice over you, and exalted the might of your foes.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath done what he thought, hath carried out his word, wherewith he gave charge in the days of old, hath thrown down, and not spared,—Thus hath he let the enemy rejoice over thee, hath raised high the horn of thine adversaries.

text.NRSV: Cry aloud to the Lord! O wall of daughter Zion! Let tears stream down like a torrent day and night! Give yourself no rest, your eyes no respite!
text.Rotherham: Their heart, hath made outcry, unto My Lord,—O wall of the daughter of Zion—Let tears run down as a torrent day and night, Do not give thyself relief, Let not the weeping of thine eye cease!

text.NRSV: Arise, cry out in the night, at the beginning of the watches! Pour out your heart like water before the presence of the Lord! Lift your hands to him for the lives of your children, who faint for hunger at the head of every street.
text.Rotherham: Arise, cry out in the night, At the beginning of the watches, pour out, like waters, thy heart, right before the face of My Lord,—Lift up, above thee, the palms of thy hands, for the life of thy children, who are swooning for hunger, at the top of all the streets!

text.NRSV: Look, O Lord, and consider! To whom have you done this? Should women eat their offspring, the children they have borne? Should priest and prophet be killed in the sanctuary of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: See, O Yahweh, and do consider, to whom thou hast acted thus severely,—Will women, devour, their own fruit—the children they have dandled? Shall priest and prophet, be slain in the sanctuary of My Lord?

text.NRSV: The young and the old are lying on the ground in the streets; my young women and my young men have fallen by the sword; in the day of your anger you have killed them, slaughtering without pity.
text.Rotherham: Youth and elder, have lain down on the ground in the streets, My virgins and my young men, have fallen by the sword,—Thou hast slain, in the day of thine anger, Thou hast slaughtered, hast not spared!

text.NRSV: You summoned, as if to a festival day, my terrors from every side; and on the day of the Lord’s anger no one escaped or survived; those whom I bore and reared my enemy has destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou proclaim, like the day of an appointed meeting—My terrors round about? when there was not—in the day of the anger of Yahweh—fugitive or survivor,—Those whom I dandled and reared, my foe hath destroyed.

text.NRSV: I am one who has seen affliction under the rod of God’s wrath;
text.Rotherham: I, am the man, that hath seen affliction, by the rod of his indignation;

text.NRSV: he has driven and brought me into darkness without any light;
text.Rotherham: Me, hath he driven out and brought into darkness, and not light;

text.NRSV: against me alone he turns his hand, again and again, all day long.
text.Rotherham: Surely, against me, doth he again and again, turn his hand all the day.

text.NRSV: He has made my flesh and my skin waste away, and broken my bones;
text.Rotherham: He hath worn out my flesh and my skin, hath broken my bones;

text.NRSV: he has besieged and enveloped me with bitterness and tribulation;
text.Rotherham: He hath built up against me, and carried round me, fortifications and a trench;

text.NRSV: he has made me live in darkness like the dead of long ago.
text.Rotherham: In dark places, hath he made me sit, like the dead of age-past times.

text.NRSV: He has walled me about so that I cannot escape; he has put heavy chains on me;
text.Rotherham: He hath walled up around me, that I cannot get out, hath weighted my fetter;

text.NRSV: though I call and cry for help, he shuts out my prayer;
text.Rotherham: Yea, when I make outcry and implore, he hath shut out my prayer;

text.NRSV: he has blocked my ways with hewn stones, he has made my paths crooked.
text.Rotherham: He hath walled in my ways with hewn stone, my paths, hath he caused to wind back.

text.NRSV: He is a bear lying in wait for me, a lion in hiding;
text.Rotherham: A bear lying in wait, he is to me, a lion, in secret places;

text.NRSV: he led me off my way and tore me to pieces; he has made me desolate;
text.Rotherham: My ways, hath he turned aside, and hath torn me in pieces, hath made me desolate;

text.NRSV: he bent his bow and set me as a mark for his arrow.
text.Rotherham: He hath trodden his bow, and set me up, as a mark for the arrow.

text.NRSV: He shot into my vitals the arrows of his quiver;
text.Rotherham: He hath caused to enter my reins, the sons of his quiver;

text.NRSV: I have become the laughingstock of all my people, the object of their taunt-songs all day long.
text.Rotherham: I have become a derision to all my people, their song all the day;

text.NRSV: He has filled me with bitterness, he has sated me with wormwood.
text.Rotherham: He hath sated me with bitter things, hath drenched me with wormwood.

text.NRSV: He has made my teeth grind on gravel, and made me cower in ashes;
text.Rotherham: And he hath crushed, with gravel-stones, my teeth, hath made me cower in ashes;

text.NRSV: my soul is bereft of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is;
text.Rotherham: And thou hast thrust away from welfare, my soul, I have forgotten prosperity;

text.NRSV: so I say, “Gone is my glory, and all that I had hoped for from the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And I said, Vanished is mine endurance, even mine expectation, from Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The thought of my affliction and my homelessness is wormwood and gall!
text.Rotherham: Remember my humiliation and my fleeings, the wormwood and poison;

text.NRSV: My soul continually thinks of it and is bowed down within me.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt, indeed remember, that, bowed down concerning myself, is my soul;

text.NRSV: But this I call to mind, and therefore I have hope:
text.Rotherham: This, will I bring back to my heart, therefore, will I hope.

text.NRSV: The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases, his mercies never come to an end;
text.Rotherham: The lovingkindnesses of Yahweh, verily they are not exhausted, Verily! not at an end, are his compassions:

text.NRSV: they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: New things for the mornings! Abundant is thy faithfulness:

text.NRSV: “The Lord is my portion,” says my soul, “therefore I will hope in him.”
text.Rotherham: My portion, is Yahweh, saith my soul, For this cause, will I wait for him.

text.NRSV: The Lord is good to those who wait for him, to the soul that seeks him.
text.Rotherham: Good is Yahweh, to them who wait for him, to the soul that will seek him;

text.NRSV: It is good that one should wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Good it is—both to wait and to be silent, for the deliverance of Yahweh;

text.NRSV: It is good for one to bear the yoke in youth,
text.Rotherham: Good it is for a man, that he should bear the yoke in his youth.

text.NRSV: to sit alone in silence when the Lord has imposed it,
text.Rotherham: Let him sit alone, and keep silence, because he took it upon himself:

text.NRSV: to put one’s mouth to the dust (there may yet be hope),
text.Rotherham: Let him put, in the dust, his mouth, peradventure, there is hope!

text.NRSV: to give one’s cheek to the smiter, and be filled with insults.
text.Rotherham: Let him give, to him that smiteth him, his cheek, let him be sated with reproach.

text.NRSV: For the Lord will not reject forever.
text.Rotherham: Surely My Lord, will not cast off, unto times age-abiding;

text.NRSV: Although he causes grief, he will have compassion according to the abundance of his steadfast love;
text.Rotherham: Surely, though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion, according to the multitude of his lovingkindnesses;

text.NRSV: for he does not willingly afflict or grieve anyone.
text.Rotherham: Surely he hath not afflicted from his heart, nor caused sorrow to the sons of men.

text.NRSV: When all the prisoners of the land are crushed under foot,
text.Rotherham: To crush, under his feet, any of the prisoners of the earth;

text.NRSV: when human rights are perverted in the presence of the Most High,
text.Rotherham: To turn aside the right of a man, before the face of the Most High;

text.NRSV: when one’s case is subverted— does the Lord not see it?
text.Rotherham: To oppress a son of earth in his cause, My Lord, hath made no provision.

text.NRSV: Who can command and have it done, if the Lord has not ordained it?
text.Rotherham: Who was it that spake, and it was done, [when], My Lord, had not commanded?

text.NRSV: Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and bad come?
text.Rotherham: Out of the mouth of the Most High, Proceed there not misfortunes and blessing?

text.NRSV: Why should any who draw breath complain about the punishment of their sins?
text.Rotherham: Why should a living son of earth complain, [Let] a man [complain] because of his sins?

text.NRSV: Let us test and examine our ways, and return to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let us search out our ways, and examine them well, and let us return unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: Let us lift up our hearts as well as our hands to God in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Let us lift up our heart, to the opened palms, to the Mighty One in the heavens;

text.NRSV: We have transgressed and rebelled, and you have not forgiven.
text.Rotherham: We, have trespassed and rebelled, Thou, hast not pardoned.

text.NRSV: You have wrapped yourself in anger and pursued us, killing without pity;
text.Rotherham: Thou hast covered thyself with anger, and pursued us, hast slain—hast not spared;

text.NRSV: you have wrapped yourself in a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast screened thyself with the clouds, that prayer, should not pass through;

text.NRSV: You have made us filth and rubbish among the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Offscouring and refuse, dost thou make us, in the midst of the peoples.

text.NRSV: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us;
text.Rotherham: With their mouth, opened wide over us, [stand] all our foes.

text.NRSV: panic and pitfall have come upon us, devastation and destruction.
text.Rotherham: Terror and a pit, have befallen us, tumult and grievous injury;

text.NRSV: My eyes flow with rivers of tears because of the destruction of my people.
text.Rotherham: With streams of water, mine eye runneth down, over the grievous injury of the daughter of my people.

text.NRSV: My eyes will flow without ceasing, without respite,
text.Rotherham: Mine eye, poureth itself out and ceaseth not, without relief;

text.NRSV: until the Lord from heaven looks down and sees.
text.Rotherham: Until Yahweh out of the heavens shall look forth, and see,

text.NRSV: My eyes cause me grief at the fate of all the young women in my city.
text.Rotherham: Mine eye dealeth severely with my soul, because of all the daughters of my city.

text.NRSV: Those who were my enemies without cause have hunted me like a bird;
text.Rotherham: They, have laid snares, for me as a bird, who are mine enemies without cause:

text.NRSV: they flung me alive into a pit and hurled stones on me;
text.Rotherham: They have cut off, in the dungeon, my life, and have cast a stone upon me;

text.NRSV: water closed over my head; I said, “I am lost.”
text.Rotherham: Waters, flowed over, my head, I said, I am cut off!

text.NRSV: I called on your name, O Lord, from the depths of the pit;
text.Rotherham: I have called upon thy Name, O Yahweh, out of the dungeon below;

text.NRSV: you heard my plea, “Do not close your ear to my cry for help, but give me relief!”
text.Rotherham: My voice, thou hast heard,—do not close thine ear to my respite, to mine outcry;

text.NRSV: You came near when I called on you; you said, “Do not fear!”
text.Rotherham: Thou drewest near, in the day I kept calling on thee, thou saidst, Do not fear!

text.NRSV: You have pleaded my cause, O Lord, you have redeemed my life.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast pleaded, O My Lord, the pleas of my soul, hast redeemed my life;

text.NRSV: You have seen the wrong done to me, O Lord; judge my cause.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast beheld, O Yahweh, my failure to get justice, Pronounce thou my sentence;

text.NRSV: You have seen all their malice, all their plots against me.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast seen all their vindictiveness, all their plots against me.

text.NRSV: You have heard their insults, O Lord, all their plots against me.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast heard their reproach, O Yahweh, all their plots against me;

text.NRSV: The whispering and the murmuring of my assailants are against me all day long.
text.Rotherham: The lips of mine assailants, and their mutterings, are against me, all the day;

text.NRSV: Whether they sit or rise—see, I am the object of their taunt-songs.
text.Rotherham: On their downsitting and their uprising, do thou look, I, am their song.

text.NRSV: Pay them back for their deeds, O Lord, according to the work of their hands!
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt render to them a recompense, O Yahweh, according to the work of their hands;

text.NRSV: Give them anguish of heart; your curse be on them!
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt suffer them a veiling of heart, thy curse to them;

text.NRSV: Pursue them in anger and destroy them from under the heavens of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt pursue in anger, and wilt destroy them, from under the heavens of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: How the gold has grown dim, how the pure gold is changed! The holy stones lie scattered at the head of every street.
text.Rotherham: How is dimmed the gold! changed the most fine gold! Poured out are the stones of the sanctuary, at the top of all the streets.

text.NRSV: The precious children of Zion, worth their weight in fine gold— how they are reckoned as earthen pots, the work of a potter’s hands!
text.Rotherham: The precious sons of Zion, they who were weighed against pure gold, How are they accounted as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!

text.NRSV: Even the jackals offer the breast and nurse their young, but my people has become cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Even wild dogs, draw out the breast, give suck to their whelps—The daughter of my people, hath become cruel, like the ostriches in the desert.

text.NRSV: The tongue of the infant sticks to the roof of its mouth for thirst; the children beg for food, but no one gives them anything.
text.Rotherham: The tongue of the suckling, cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst,—Young children, have asked bread, there was none, to break, it to them.

text.NRSV: Those who feasted on delicacies perish in the streets; those who were brought up in purple cling to ash heaps.
text.Rotherham: They who used to eat delicacies, are deserted in the streets,—They who used to be carried on crimson, have embraced heaps of refuse.

text.NRSV: For the chastisement of my people has been greater than the punishment of Sodom, which was overthrown in a moment, though no hand was laid on it.
text.Rotherham: And the punishment of the daughter of my people hath grown greater than the punishment of Sodom,—which was overthrown as in a moment, when no hands had been laid violently upon her.

text.NRSV: Her princes were purer than snow, whiter than milk; their bodies were more ruddy than coral, their hair like sapphire.
text.Rotherham: Purer were her Nazirites than snow, whiter were they than milk,—more ruddy, in body, than coral, A sapphire, was their beauty of form.

text.NRSV: Now their appearance is blacker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become as dry as wood.
text.Rotherham: Darker than a coal, is their visage, They are not known in the streets—Their skin shrivelleth on their bones, is withered, become like a stick.

text.NRSV: Happier were those pierced by the sword than those pierced by hunger, whose life drains away, unstabbed, for lack of the fruits of the field.
text.Rotherham: Better are the slain of the sword, than the slain of the famine,—for, these, pine away, stricken through, wanting the produce of the field.

text.NRSV: The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children; they became their food in the destruction of my people.
text.Rotherham: The hands of compassionate women, have cooked their own children,—they have served as nourishment to them, in the grievous injury of the daughter of my people.

text.NRSV: The Lord gave full vent to his wrath; he poured out his hot anger, and kindled a fire in Zion that consumed its foundations.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath completed his indignation, hath poured out the glow of his anger; and hath kindled a fire in Zion, which hath devoured her foundations.

text.NRSV: The kings of the earth did not believe, nor did any of the inhabitants of the world, that foe or enemy could enter the gates of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Neither the kings of the earth, nor any of the inhabitants of the world, believed, that an adversary or an enemy, should enter, the gates of Jerusalem!

text.NRSV: It was for the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests, who shed the blood of the righteous in the midst of her.
text.Rotherham: [It is] for the sins of her prophets, the iniquities of her priests,—who have been pouring out, in her midst, the blood of the righteous!

text.NRSV: Blindly they wandered through the streets, so defiled with blood that no one was able to touch their garments.
text.Rotherham: They have wandered—blind—in the streets, have defiled themselves with blood; so that men may not touch their garments.

text.NRSV: “Away! Unclean!” people shouted at them; “Away! Away! Do not touch!” So they became fugitives and wanderers; it was said among the nations, “They shall stay here no longer.”
text.Rotherham: Turn aside! Unclean! have they cried to them, Turn aside! Turn aside! Do not touch! because they have fled, yea, wandered, Men said among the nations, They will not again tarry;

text.NRSV: The Lord himself has scattered them, he will regard them no more; no honor was shown to the priests, no favor to the elders.
text.Rotherham: The face of Yahweh, hath scattered them, He will look them out no more: The persons of the priests, they have not respected, To the elders, have they shown no favour.

text.NRSV: Our eyes failed, ever watching vainly for help; we were watching eagerly for a nation that could not save.
text.Rotherham: Silly shall our eyes fail, for our help that is vain: In our watchtower, have we watched—for a nation that will not save.

text.NRSV: They dogged our steps so that we could not walk in our streets; our end drew near; our days were numbered; for our end had come.
text.Rotherham: They have laid snares for our steps, that we cannot walk in our own broadways: Drawn near hath our end, Fulfilled are our days, yea arrived hath our end.

text.NRSV: Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles in the heavens; they chased us on the mountains, they lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Swifter are our pursuers, than the eagles of the heavens,—Over the mountains, have they come hotly after us, In the wilderness, have they lain in wait for us.

text.NRSV: The Lord’s anointed, the very breath of our life, was caught in their pits— the one of whom we said, “Under his shadow we shall live among the nations.”
text.Rotherham: The fragrance of our nostrils, The Anointed of Yahweh, hath been captured in their pits,—of whom we had said—In his shade, shall we live among the nations.

text.NRSV: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter Edom, you who live in the land of Uz; but to you also the cup shall pass; you shall become drunk and strip yourself bare.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, thou inhabitress in the and of Uz,—Even unto thee, shall the cup pass along, thou shalt be drunken, and expose thy shame.

text.NRSV: The punishment of your iniquity, O daughter Zion, is accomplished, he will keep you in exile no longer; but your iniquity, O daughter Edom, he will punish, he will expose your sins.
text.Rotherham: Completed is thy punishment, O daughter of Zion, He will no more carry thee away captive—He hath punished thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom, he hath stripped the veil from off thy sins.

text.NRSV: Remember, O Lord, what has befallen us; look, and see our disgrace!
text.Rotherham: Remember, O Yahweh, what hath befallen us, Look around, and see our reproach:

text.NRSV: Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers, our homes to aliens.
text.Rotherham: Our inheritance, turned over to foreigners, our houses, to aliens.

text.NRSV: We have become orphans, fatherless; our mothers are like widows.
text.Rotherham: Orphans, have we become, and fatherless, our mothers, are widows indeed.

text.NRSV: We must pay for the water we drink; the wood we get must be bought.
text.Rotherham: Our water—for silver, have we drunk, our wood—for a price, cometh in.

text.NRSV: With a yoke on our necks we are hard driven; we are weary, we are given no rest.
text.Rotherham: Upon our necks, are our pursuers, We labour, and there is allowed us no rest.

text.NRSV: We have made a pact with Egypt and Assyria, to get enough bread.
text.Rotherham: To Egypt, have we stretched out our hand, to Assyria, to be satisfied with bread.

text.NRSV: Our ancestors sinned; they are no more, and we bear their iniquities.
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, sinned, and are not, and, we, their iniquities, have borne.

text.NRSV: Slaves rule over us, and there is no one to deliver us from their hand.
text.Rotherham: Slaves, have ruled over us, There is none to set free from their hand.

text.NRSV: We get our bread at the peril of our lives, because of the sword in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: At the risk of our life, do we bring in our bread, because of the sword of the desert.

text.NRSV: Our skin is black as an oven from the scorching heat of famine.
text.Rotherham: Our skin, as with a furnace, is scorched, because of the hot winds of famine.

text.NRSV: Women are raped in Zion, virgins in the towns of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Women—in Zion, were ravished, virgins, in the cities of Judah!

text.NRSV: Princes are hung up by their hands; no respect is shown to the elders.
text.Rotherham: Princes, by their hand, have been hanged, The faces of elders, not honoured.

text.NRSV: Young men are made to grind at the mill, and boys stagger under loads of wood.
text.Rotherham: Young men, a millstone, have lifted, and, youths, under wood, have staggered.

text.NRSV: The old men have left the city gate, the young men their music.
text.Rotherham: Elders, from the gates, have ceased. Young men, from their music.

text.NRSV: The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has been turned to mourning.
text.Rotherham: Ceased hath the joy of our hearts, Changed to mourning, our dance.

text.NRSV: The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us, for we have sinned!
text.Rotherham: Fallen is the crown of our head. Surely woe to us, for we have sinned.

text.NRSV: Because of this our hearts are sick, because of these things our eyes have grown dim:
text.Rotherham: For this cause, faint is our heart, For these things, dimmed are our eyes:

text.NRSV: because of Mount Zion, which lies desolate; jackals prowl over it.
text.Rotherham: Because of Mount Zion, which is desolate, jackals, have gone prowling therein.

text.NRSV: But you, O Lord, reign forever; your throne endures to all generations.
text.Rotherham: Thou, O Yahweh, unto times age-abiding, dost remain, Thy throne, from generation to generation:

text.NRSV: Why have you forgotten us completely? Why have you forsaken us these many days?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore shouldst thou perpetually forget us? forsake us, to length of days?

text.NRSV: Restore us to yourself, O Lord, that we may be restored; renew our days as of old—
text.Rotherham: Bring us back, O Yahweh, unto thyself, and we will come back! Renew our days, as of old;

text.NRSV: unless you have utterly rejected us, and are angry with us beyond measure.
text.Rotherham: For though thou hast not, utterly rejected, us, thou art wroth with us—exceedingly!
